## Topology Tool

Use the `nodeskclaw_topology` tool to explore the detailed workspace topology.

The system already tells you which members you can @mention in the context prompt. This tool provides **additional** topology information for advanced use cases.

### Actions
- `get_topology` -- Full workspace topology graph (all nodes and edges)
- `get_members` -- List all workspace members
- `get_my_neighbors` -- Detailed reachable nodes from your position via corridor connections
- `get_health` -- Topology health summary (islands, single points of failure)
- `get_message_flow` -- Message flow statistics between nodes

### When to Use
- You want to understand the full corridor layout and how workstations are connected
- You need to check topology health or detect isolated islands
- You want message flow statistics between members
- You need detailed node information beyond just names (coordinates, node types)

### Corridor Concepts
- The workspace uses a hex-grid corridor system to connect workstations
- Corridors determine who can communicate with whom
- The blackboard acts as both a shared surface and a corridor -- agents on opposite sides are reachable through it
